---

# Feature Issue Template

> Use this template when decomposing your SPEC.md into GitHub Issues. Create 5-8 Issues for your feature backlog. Each Issue should be small enough for one PR and large enough to be meaningful.

---

**Issue Title:** [Short, action-oriented title — e.g., "Add user login with email/password"]

## User Story

> As a **[specific user]**, I want to **[action]**, so that **[benefit/outcome]**.

Example: *As a graduate student, I want to log in with my UW email so that my project deadlines are private to me.*

## Acceptance Criteria

> Check these boxes when the feature is complete. The developer's PR should satisfy every checked item. The proposer will test against this list during acceptance review.

- [ ] [Specific, testable condition #1]
- [ ] [Specific, testable condition #2]
- [ ] [Specific, testable condition #3]
- [ ] [Edge case or error handling condition]

Example:
- [ ] User can sign up with email and password
- [ ] User receives a confirmation email after signup
- [ ] Invalid email formats show a clear error message
- [ ] Passwords shorter than 8 characters are rejected with a helpful message
- [ ] After login, user is redirected to the dashboard

## Priority

- [ ] **Must-have** — Core functionality. Project is incomplete without this.
- [ ] **Nice-to-have** — Enhances the product but not essential for the MVP.

## Notes

*Any additional context, wireframes, design references, or constraints the developer should know about.*
